L1.area: Data partitioning and batch processing utilities

L2.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in a chunking utility where the length of the input data is cast to a 16-bit unsigned integer. When the input contains more than 65535 elements, the cast wraps around, causing the chunking loop to process only a truncated portion of the data or panic due to out-of-bounds slice access. This leads to incomplete processing of permission checks and related operations.

L3.area: Generic slice chunking utilities used by the dispatch layer
L3.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in a generic slice chunking helper where the length of the input data is cast to a 16-bit unsigned integer before being used to compute chunk boundaries. When the input slice contains more than 65535 elements, the cast wraps around, causing the chunking loop to iterate over a truncated range. This results in permission check dispatches silently missing elements or panicking on out-of-bounds slice access when resources have very wide relation sets.

ground_truth.reason: The ForEachChunkUntil function casts len(data) to uint16, which overflows when the slice has more than 65535 elements. This causes the chunking loop to miss elements or panic, resulting in denied permission checks that should be allowed.
